metadata.title: ctDNA MRD Detection — The Blood Test That Sees Cancer Before Imaging Can
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E body text; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral

skin_warnings: B00: palette=claude but the cold open is 'NikBearBrownOpen' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk

beats.narration_text: Jambo, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. ctDNA MRD — the blood test that sees cancer before imaging can.

After surgery for colorectal cancer, the scan is clean. But a ctDNA blood test taken six weeks later is positive. That patient will almost certainly relapse within two years — and the ctDNA-negative patient probably won't. A biomarker that detects micrometastatic disease invisible to imaging already exists. Should every post-surgical patient get it?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: After surgery for colorectal cancer, the scan is clean. But a ctDNA blood test taken six weeks later is positive. That patient will almost certainly relapse wit

beats.narration_text: Research ctDNA minimal residual disease testing in solid tumors: which cancer types have strongest validation, sensitivity and specificity of leading platforms, lead time to imaging, and trials showing ctDNA-MRD-guided adjuvant therapy changes outcomes.

props.command: claude "Research ctDNA minimal residual disease testing in solid tumors: which cancer types have the strongest clinical validation (colorectal, breast, lung, bladder), what is the sensitivity and specificity of the leading platforms (Foundation Medicine, Guardant, Grail), what is the lead time to imaging detection, and which clinical trials have shown ctDNA-MRD-guided escalation/de-escalation of adjuvant therapy changes outcomes?"

beats.narration_text: Python script building evidence matrix — cancer type versus platform sensitivity versus clinical trial evidence.

props.code:
DATA = [
    {"cancer": "Colorectal (stage II)", "sensitivity": 0.88, "lead_mo": 8, "rct": "DYNAMIC (NEJM 2022)"},
    {"cancer": "Breast (early)",        "sensitivity": 0.70, "lead_mo": 11, "rct": "C-TRAK (ongoing)"},
    {"cancer": "NSCLC (stage I-III)",   "sensitivity": 0.75, "lead_mo": 5,  "rct": "TRACERx (Nature 2022)"},
    {"cancer": "Bladder",               "sensitivity": 0.82, "lead_mo": 3,  "rct": "Pilot only"},
    {"cancer": "Ovarian",               "sensitivity": 0.60, "lead_mo": 2,  "rct": "No RCT"},
]
print(f"{'Cancer':<28} {'Sens':>5}  {'Lead (mo)':>10}  {'Trial evidence'}")
print("-" * 65)
for d in DATA:
    flag = "  <- VALIDATED" if "NEJM" in d["rct"] or "Nature" in d["rct"] else ""
    print(f"{d['cancer']:<28} {d['sensitivity']*100:>4.0f}%  {d['lead_mo']:>9}mo  {d['rct']}{flag}")

beats.narration_text: Evidence matrix output — cancer types with sensitivity, lead time months, and trial evidence tier.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Evidence matrix output — cancer types with sensitivity, lead time months, and trial evidence tier.

beats.narration_text: Add a column: for each cancer type, what is the cost per test and is it covered by Medicare or major private insurers as of 2024?

beats.narration_text: Revised matrix with cost and coverage column — what the test costs and who pays for it.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Revised matrix with cost and coverage column — what the test costs and who pays for it.

beats.narration_text: The test works. The clinical question is not whether ctDNA detects MRD — it does, months before imaging. The question is whether acting on that detection improves survival. The DYNAMIC trial said yes for colorectal cancer. We need equivalents for every cancer type before this becomes standard of care.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The test works. The clinical question is not whether ctDNA detects MRD — it does, months before imaging. The question is whether acting on that detection improv

beats.narration_text: Read the DYNAMIC trial by Tie et al. 2022 in NEJM. Count the number of patients who avoided adjuvant chemotherapy based on ctDNA negativity — and the recurrence rate in that group. That is the number you need to trust the test.

beats.narration_text: Liam, in for Bear. Build it, then take it apart.
